skill.name: architecture-synthesis
skill.description: Generate reference architecture specifications for agent frameworks, including primitives, protocols, execution loops, diagrams, and implementation roadmaps.
skill.summary: Create a structured architecture specification for a new agent framework.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, ordinary architecture terminology, Mermaid examples, and artifact path examples; no executable command, network, secret access, or prompt injection behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown code block delimiters around Python, pseudocode, Mermaid, and output tree examples. They are not shell backtick execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens are visible Markdown fence delimiters. The file contains documentation examples only, with no runnable script wrapper or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary words in the architecture text, such as description and design. The skill does not define or recommend cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain architecture or interface wording, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or algorithm selection appears in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections occur in Mermaid diagram and output artifact examples. They do not instruct the assistant to inspect the host system or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are a Mermaid code fence and static diagram filenames. No system discovery command or host data collection instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear architecture decisions before building agent frameworks. This skill turns prior framework analysis into a structured specification with primitives, protocols, loops, diagrams, and a roadmap.
actual_capabilities: Defines core Message, AgentState, ToolResult, AgentFinish, and AgentContinue primitives.
Specifies LLM, Tool, and Memory protocol interfaces for an agent framework.
Drafts an execution loop with step limits, tool execution, state updates, and events.
Creates a Mermaid architecture diagram connecting core, protocol, execution, and integration layers.
Produces an implementation roadmap with core, robustness, extensibility, and production phases.
limitations: It provides architecture guidance, not a complete production framework.
It assumes comparative analysis and requirements already exist.
The Python examples are conceptual and need implementation review.
Provider integrations, persistence, and security controls need separate design work.

use_cases.title: Start a New Agent Framework
use_cases.description: Convert research findings into a practical architecture specification before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Interfaces
use_cases.description: Define shared LLM, Tool, and Memory contracts so teams can build compatible components.

use_cases.title: Plan Implementation Phases
use_cases.description: Create a phased roadmap that separates core behavior, reliability work, extensions, and production hardening.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first architecture draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-synthesis skill to create a reference architecture for a simple agent framework. Include primitives, protocols, an execution loop, and a roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Design Framework Primitives
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused request for core type definitions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-synthesis skill to define the core Message, State, Result, and Tool primitives for my agent framework. Explain each responsibility clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize from Prior Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using existing research artifacts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-synthesis skill with my comparative matrix and anti-pattern catalog. Produce decisions, interface protocols, execution flow, diagrams, and implementation phases.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Implementation Blueprint
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a build-ready specification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the architecture-synthesis skill to produce a build-ready architecture blueprint for a production agent framework. Include extension points, failure handling, observability needs, and milestone sequencing.

output_examples.input: Design a reference architecture for a minimal tool-using agent framework.
output: A primitive model defining immutable messages, copy-on-write state, tool results, and step outcomes.
Protocol sections for LLM generation, tool execution, and memory context retrieval.
A roadmap split into core implementation, robustness, extensibility, and production readiness.

output_examples.input: Turn our framework comparison into a target architecture.
output: A decision-led architecture that preserves strong patterns and avoids listed anti-patterns.
A layered diagram plan covering core types, protocol interfaces, execution flow, and integrations.
Implementation artifacts such as architecture notes, primitive specifications, protocol documentation, examples, and a roadmap.

best_practices: Provide a comparative matrix, anti-pattern catalog, and design requirements before synthesis.
Review generated protocols with implementers before locking interface contracts.
Treat the output as a specification draft that needs testing, security review, and production validation.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without prior requirements or framework analysis.
Do not copy conceptual examples directly into production without review.
Do not treat the roadmap as a substitute for project planning and risk assessment.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a reference architecture specification for an agent framework, including primitives, protocols, execution flow, diagrams, and roadmap phases.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready code?
faq.answer: No. It provides conceptual Python templates and design guidance that require implementation, testing, and review.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use a comparative framework matrix, an anti-pattern catalog, and clear design requirements.

faq.question: Can it help with Claude and Codex projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure architecture work for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that involve agent framework design.

faq.question: Does it choose a specific LLM provider?
faq.answer: No. It defines provider-neutral protocols and shows where integrations such as OpenAI or Anthropic can fit.

faq.question: Is security design included?
faq.answer: Only at a high level. Authentication, sandboxing, secret handling, and threat modeling need separate dedicated review.
